_note: SEED CONTENT — every Yorùbá string must be reviewed by a native speaker before release. Audio fields are filled once native-speaker recordings exist.

exercises.note: Mid–mid tone: ọkọ = husband. Tone changes the meaning!
Mid–low tone: ọkọ̀ = vehicle/canoe. Compare ọkọ (husband).
Low–low: ìgbà = time. Same letters, different melody.
The Ẹ shows respect — use it for elders and groups.
Ẹ ṣé to elders/groups; O ṣé to friends.
ọ (with the dot) sounds like 'aw' in 'law'; o sounds like 'o' in 'go'.
ẹja (fish) vs ẹran (meat) — both start with ẹ.
àmàlà — all-low tone, a yam-flour staple.
rà (buy) and tà (sell) differ only by the first consonant.
mélòó (how many, counting) vs èló (how much, price).
àwọn also means "the" for plural nouns.
